codeReview.summary: All 14 place coordinates were correctly updated in data/places.ts to match the US Census Geocoder values specified in the feature description. The diff is clean: exactly 28 insertions and 28 deletions (14 places × 2 coordinates each), modifying only lat/lng values with no unintended side effects. All corrected coordinates fall within the existing test bounds (40.68–40.715 lat, -73.945 to -73.89 lng), so no test changes were needed. Tests (129) pass and build succeeds.

issues.description: Minor cosmetic inconsistency: Nook's coordinates use 4 decimal places (40.7049, -73.9246) while all other corrected places use 5 decimal places. This is because JavaScript drops trailing zeros from numeric literals (40.70490 → 40.7049). Numerically identical to the specified values, no functional impact.
Same trailing-zero issue for Bushniwa's lng: -73.9341 instead of -73.93410. Numerically identical, cosmetic only.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The fullstack-worker skill procedure step 2 says 'Write failing tests first (RED)' unconditionally. For pure data correction features like this one (updating coordinate values), TDD doesn't apply since no new behavior is being added. The worker correctly skipped this step. The skill could clarify that TDD applies to new functionality, not data corrections.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill SKILL.md step 2 vs transcript showing worker went straight to edits after reading context files. skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true, no deviations noted.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker skipped manual verification with agent-browser/curl (skill step 5). For a coordinate-only data fix with the known WebGL limitation in headless Chrome, there's nothing meaningful to verify visually. This is a reasonable deviation that didn't affect quality.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton shows no dev server start or agent-browser usage. Handoff verification only lists pnpm test, pnpm build, and git diff.

summary: Clean pass. All 14 place coordinates correctly updated to match US Census Geocoder values. Diff is minimal and precise (1 file, 28 insertions/deletions). All coordinates verified to fall within existing test bounds. Tests and build pass. Two non-blocking cosmetic issues with trailing-zero precision in JavaScript numeric literals (Nook and Bushniwa), which are inherent to the language and have zero functional impact.
